test_cases.title: Export task data for a project with multiple tasks produces a valid JSON file
preconditions: User is authenticated and has access to the target project
The target project contains at least two tasks with varied field values
The documented API response schema for the tasks list endpoint is available for comparison
steps: Navigate to the target project
Trigger the export action for the project's task data
Download the resulting file
Open and parse the downloaded file as JSON
Validate the JSON structure against the documented API response schema for the tasks list endpoint
Verify each task entry contains all required fields defined in the schema
test_cases.expected_result: The downloaded file is valid JSON, contains all tasks present in the project, and every field and structure conforms to the documented API response schema for the tasks list endpoint.

test_cases.title: Export task data for a project with no tasks produces an empty JSON array
preconditions: User is authenticated and has access to the target project
The target project exists and contains zero tasks
steps: Navigate to the target project that has no tasks
Trigger the export action for the project's task data
Download the resulting file
Open and parse the downloaded file as JSON
test_cases.expected_result: The downloaded file is valid JSON containing exactly an empty array (`[]`) and no other top-level content.

test_cases.title: Exported filename matches the required pattern '{project-name}-tasks-{date}.json'
preconditions: User is authenticated and has access to the target project
The target project has a known name (e.g., 'my-project')
The current date is known before initiating the export
steps: Note the current date in the expected format (format to be confirmed per implementation)
Navigate to the target project named 'my-project'
Trigger the export action for the project's task data
Inspect the filename of the downloaded file
test_cases.expected_result: The downloaded file is named 'my-project-tasks-{today's date}.json', where the date matches the date at the time of export and the project name matches the project's name exactly.

test_cases.title: Exported file contains only the tasks belonging to the specified project and not tasks from other projects
preconditions: At least two projects exist, each containing distinct tasks
steps: Note the task IDs/titles in the target project
Note the task IDs/titles in a different project
Navigate to the target project
Trigger the export action for the project's task data
Download and parse the resulting JSON file
Check the exported task records against tasks from both projects
test_cases.expected_result: The exported JSON contains only the tasks belonging to the target project; no tasks from any other project are present in the file.

test_cases.title: Unauthenticated user cannot export project task data
preconditions: No active user session exists (user is logged out)
A valid project with tasks exists in the system
steps: Without logging in, attempt to trigger the export action for a known project's task data (e.g., via direct API call or UI navigation)
Observe the system response
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies the export request and does not return or download any JSON file; an appropriate authentication error (e.g., HTTP 401) is returned.

test_cases.title: Exported JSON file is rejected when its structure deviates from the API schema (schema validation negative check)
preconditions: A previously exported JSON file is available
The documented API response schema for the tasks list endpoint is available
steps: Take the exported JSON file and manually alter it to remove a required field defined in the API response schema
Run the modified file through a JSON schema validator against the documented API response schema
Observe the validation result
test_cases.expected_result: The schema validator reports a validation error, confirming that a file missing required fields does not conform to the documented schema — thereby validating the schema enforcement check used in positive test cases.

assumptions: The exact date format used in the filename (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD) is not specified in the requirement; test cases assume the format is defined in implementation documentation and should be verified against it.

The project name used in the filename is the project's display/slug name as stored in the system; special characters or spaces in project names may require sanitization, but no handling rule is stated.
The export action is accessible to authenticated users who have at least read access to the project; role-based access control beyond authentication is not specified.
The export is triggered synchronously and results in a direct file download; asynchronous export with a separate download link is not assumed.
The 'date' in the filename refers to the date of the export action, not a task-related date.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the exact date format for the filename pattern, which must be clarified to make filename tests deterministic; (2) how special characters or whitespace in project names are handled in the filename; (3) whether users with restricted roles (e.g., viewer vs. editor) have different export permissions; (4) whether the export includes soft-deleted or archived tasks; (5) the encoding of the JSON file (e.g., UTF-8). Coverage gap: no performance or large-dataset test cases are included because no scale requirements are stated. The schema conformance test relies on an external documented schema whose location and version are not referenced in the requirement.
